behavior_summary: When the player examines the GATESKUL object/NPC, the U8P_GATESKUL::look handler displays a single short description text 'skull ' and then suspends/pauses the U8P_GATESKUL::look process.
There are no menu choices, conditionals, external calls, or global flag reads/writes involved; the behavior is minimal and deterministic.
The suspend at the end indicates the engine will wait for player acknowledgement or resume before finishing the U8P_GATESKUL::look process.
notable_control_flow: Single straight-line flow: emit a bark 'skull ' then suspend; no branches or loops.
Execution is driven by the engine's U8P_GATESKUL::look/examine process (process type 0x0208) and runs immediately when invoked.
No internal or external calls alter state; control returns to the engine only after the suspend completes.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the text 'skull ' intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
Is this object referenced by any fast-area triggers or other scripts that perform additional behavior beyond the U8P_GATESKUL::look handler?
Does the suspend represent a short UI pause for the player or a longer process wait used by other systems (inventory, dialogues)?

main_function.name: U8P_GATESKUL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text shown when the player uses the 'U8P_GATESKUL::look' (examine) action on the GATESKUL entity and then pause the U8P_GATESKUL::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player examines/looks at the GATESKUL object (engine U8P_GATESKUL::look/examine action).

function_map.name: U8P_GATESKUL::look
function_map.role: Handle U8P_GATESKUL::look/examine for the GATESKUL entity: display a short description and suspend the process.
